text.title: GitLab Board Types, Scoped Labels, and WIP Limits
text.summary: Rules for configuring GitLab Issue Boards with scoped labels and WIP limits for state-machine semantics.

section.title: Board Types

rule.statement: Use project-level boards for single-repo workflows; use group-level boards for cross-project visibility in multi-team programs.
rule.rationale: Group boards aggregate issues across subgroups but inherit labels inconsistently — agents moving issues across groups can lose label context.

section.p: Board list types available: Label (workflow stages), Assignee (workload view), Milestone (release planning), Iteration (sprint board). Multiple boards per project requires GitLab Premium.

rule.statement: Use "::" syntax for all workflow, priority, and type labels to enforce mutual exclusivity — an issue can hold only one label per scope prefix.
rule.rationale: Without scoped labels, an issue can carry both "workflow::backlog" and "workflow::in-progress" simultaneously, corrupting board state.

example.note: Scoped labels are mutually exclusive within their prefix. An issue transitions by swapping the label (e.g., workflow::backlog replaced by workflow::in-progress).

rule.statement: Keep total label count under 25 per project; archive or rename rather than accumulate.
rule.rationale: Label sprawl makes triage harder and slows label picker rendering in the UI.

rule.statement: Set WIP limits at the team's measured throughput, not aspirational capacity; tighten if cycle-time degrades.
rule.rationale: WIP limits backed by observed data are defensible; aspirational limits get ignored under pressure.

example.code: board_configuration: name: "Development Board" lists: - type: label label: "workflow::backlog" position: 0 - type: label label: "workflow::ready" position: 1 limit: 10 - type: label label: "workflow::in-progress" position: 2 limit: 5 - type: label label: "workflow::review" position: 3 limit: 3 - type: label label: "workflow::testing" position: 4 - type: closed position: 5 filters: milestone: "v1.0" labels: ["type::feature"]

rule.statement: Issue and MR templates must be version-controlled in ".gitlab/" and reviewed via MR like code, not edited through the UI.
rule.rationale: UI-only edits produce unreviewed changes to team process with no audit trail.

rule.statement: Use GitLab Iteration cadences with automatic creation for sprints; do not create iterations manually one by one.
rule.rationale: Cadence automation ensures consistent sprint length and reduces admin overhead.

example.code: iteration_cadence: title: "Development Sprints" start_date: "2024-01-01" duration: 2 # weeks upcoming: 4 automatic: true
